title: tetratheca ericifolia var. pilosa
intro: Tetratheca ericifolia var. pilosa, also known as the hairy pink-pea, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for wound healing and treating infections. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the hairy pink-pea plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
